grounding_note: Row 269 (caseworker discharge decision after a stroke) split humans between General Litigation and Malpractice (Medical); row 428 split between Child Abuse Victims and Malpractice (Legal). The exact route depends on whether the harm flowed from a licensed professional's judgment within a professional relationship — a fact users bury inside narrative grievances. Includes the dental/legal variants and the row 62/266 animal-injury boundary.

scenarios.opening_query: My mother was sent home from care way too early after her stroke and got much worse. Who answers for that?
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > General Litigation || General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The discharge decision was made by her attending physician against the physical therapist's written recommendation.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Her own doctor ordered the discharge even though the physical therapist recommended against it in writing.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a licensed medical provider's treatment decision caused the harm
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The doctors wanted her to stay; it was the insurance company's caseworker who forced the transfer to a cheaper facility.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The doctors wanted her to stay — the insurance caseworker forced the move to a cheaper place.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 269's precise ambiguity: physician judgment (malpractice) vs administrative caseworker decision (not).

scenarios.opening_query: I lost my case because of things that went wrong that were never my fault, and I want accountability.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > General Litigation || General Litigation > Malpractice (Legal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: My own attorney missed the filing deadline, and the judge dismissed my case because of it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My own lawyer blew the filing deadline — that's why the judge threw my case out.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user's own attorney's error caused the loss
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My lawyer did fine; the other side hid documents we only discovered after the verdict.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: My lawyer was fine — the other side hid documents we found after the verdict.
scenarios.vetting_note: Missed deadline is the taxonomy's own legal-malpractice example; opening hides who erred.

scenarios.opening_query: A dog bit me badly on my forearm and I've lost months of work. I still have nerve damage.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Animal Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: The bigger fight is that animal control ruled the dog 'potentially dangerous' and the owner is appealing to keep it next door — I want it declared dangerous and restrained.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Animal control called it 'potentially dangerous' and the owner's appealing — I want the dangerous-dog ruling upheld and the dog restrained.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user seeks injury compensation or an animal-control legal determination
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I only want compensation for my medical bills and lost wages from the owner.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: What I want is compensation for the bills and lost wages.
scenarios.vetting_note: Rows 62/266: one human chose PI, models added Animal Law; the remedy sought is the honest disambiguator.

scenarios.opening_query: My root canal was botched so badly I ended up in the ER and lost the tooth next to it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Malpractice (Dental)
scenarios.hidden_fact: A second dentist's records show the first dentist drilled into the wrong canal and missed the infection on the X-ray.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The new dentist's records show the first one drilled the wrong canal and missed the infection on the X-ray.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a dentist's professional error below the standard of care caused the injury
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The dental work itself was fine — I was hurt when their exam chair collapsed under me in the waiting area.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The dental work was fine — their chair collapsed under me, that's how I got hurt.
scenarios.vetting_note: Professional-error vs premises accident inside the same clinic; the mechanism of harm is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: The surgery center where I had my knee done left me with an infection that put me back in the hospital for weeks.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || General Litigation > Wrongful Death
scenarios.hidden_fact: I'm actually asking for my brother — he had the same procedure there, got the same infection, and passed away in June. Our family wants to act.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's really about my brother — same procedure, same infection, and he passed away in June. The family wants to pursue it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the injured patient survived or the family is bringing a death claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I recovered, but the infection cost me my job and months of therapy, and I want the center held responsible.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I recovered, but it cost me my job and months of therapy — I want them held responsible.
scenarios.vetting_note: Death of the patient is the single fact that changes the cause of action; families often open with the survivor's framing.

scenarios.opening_query: I want to sue my old lawyer over how my divorce ended up. I got a terrible result.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Legal) || General Litigation > General Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: Looking at it honestly, he didn't make an error — my real complaint is my ex hid assets we're only finding now, and I want that reopened.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Honestly my lawyer didn't mess up — my ex hid assets we're just now finding, and I want the case reopened.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the attorney committed an identifiable error or the grievance lies elsewhere
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He never told me about the settlement offer that would have kept my house — I found the email later.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He never told me about the settlement offer that would've saved my house — I found the email afterward.
scenarios.vetting_note: Anger at outcome vs identifiable professional error; the specific-error fact separates the routes.

scenarios.opening_query: The pharmacy gave my father something that put him in intensive care for a week.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || General Litigation > Products Liability
scenarios.hidden_fact: The pharmacist filled the prescription at ten times the prescribed dose — the bottle label shows it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The pharmacist dispensed ten times the prescribed dose — you can see it right on the bottle.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a licensed professional's dispensing error caused the harm
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The dose was right but the manufacturer recalled that batch for contamination two weeks later.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The dose was right — but the manufacturer recalled that exact batch for contamination.
scenarios.vetting_note: Dispensing error (professional negligence) vs contaminated batch (products liability): same injury, cleanly split by the disclosure.

scenarios.opening_query: Something went wrong during my wife's outpatient procedure and nobody at the clinic will give us straight answers.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || General Litigation > Health Law/HIPAA
scenarios.hidden_fact: She recovered fine — what we discovered is the clinic shared her full chart with her employer without any authorization.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She's physically fine — the real problem is the clinic sent her whole chart to her employer without permission.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the grievance is a treatment injury or an unauthorized records disclosure
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The anesthesia was mismanaged and she has permanent numbness in her hand.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They mismanaged the anesthesia — her hand is permanently numb.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Something went wrong at the clinic' spans bodily injury and privacy breach; the harm type is the fact.

scenarios.opening_query: Our financial advisor churned my parents' retirement account and they lost a huge part of their savings.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > General Litigation || Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse
scenarios.hidden_fact: He's also my father's nephew, got himself added as a signer on their accounts, and moved money to himself while dad's dementia progressed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's actually my dad's nephew — he got signing authority and moved money to himself while dad's dementia got worse.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a trusted person exploited an elder's diminished capacity
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's an unrelated licensed advisor at a brokerage; the losses came from excessive trading for commissions.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's just their broker — no relation — and the losses were from constant trading for commissions.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 136's churning case; the family-insider-with-capacity-decline fact reroutes it to elder financial abuse.

scenarios.opening_query: My therapist did something during our sessions that I believe crossed every professional line.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical) || General Litigation > Health Law/HIPAA || General Litigation > General Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: She discussed my diagnosis and session details with my ex-wife, who she also treats — my ex quoted my own words back to me in the custody case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She told my ex-wife — another patient of hers — my diagnosis and what I said in session. My ex quoted me in the custody case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the professional breach is a confidentiality violation or treatment harm
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: She kept me on a treatment plan the licensing board later found harmful and below professional standards, and I deteriorated badly under it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: She kept me on a treatment plan the board itself later called harmful — I got much worse under it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Confidentiality breach vs negligent treatment: distinct exact labels behind one 'crossed the line' framing.

scenarios.opening_query: The vet's office is responsible for what happened to my dog and I'm not letting it go.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Animal Law || General Litigation > Property Damage
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's not about treatment — their kennel tech left the gate open, my dog ran into the road and was killed by a car. I want them to answer for the loss.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It wasn't treatment — their tech left the kennel gate open and my dog was hit by a car and killed.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the loss came from professional treatment or ordinary negligence in handling
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The vet operated on the wrong leg and now my dog can barely walk — I'm disputing the surgery itself.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The vet operated on the wrong leg — my dog can barely walk now.
scenarios.vetting_note: Law treats animals as property; handling negligence vs veterinary judgment routes differently. Honest, sad, common.

scenarios.opening_query: I was injured by a chiropractor's adjustment and my regular doctor says the damage may be permanent.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Personal Injury || General Litigation > Malpractice (Medical)
scenarios.hidden_fact: A neurologist's report says the neck manipulation was contraindicated given the artery condition in my chart, which the chiropractor had.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The neurologist says that neck manipulation was contraindicated for my artery condition — and it was right there in my chart.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the licensed provider deviated from the professional standard of care
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The adjustment was fine — I was hurt when the treatment table's headrest collapsed mid-session.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The adjustment itself was fine — the table's headrest collapsed under me mid-session.
scenarios.vetting_note: Standard-of-care documentation vs equipment failure; parallel structure to 04 in a different profession.
